title: Gate OpenViking hierarchy selection scoring on scored hierarchy output

encoding.reason: OpenViking hierarchy selection is encoded as a benchmark job, but scoring is blocked until the adapter emits selected hierarchy nodes with evidence ids after the same-corpus precondition passes.

follow_up.title: Materialize OpenViking selected hierarchy nodes
follow_up.reason: The context-trajectory adapter must return selected parent, child, and resource nodes with evidence ids before hierarchy quality can be scored against ELF.

items.text: A scored OpenViking hierarchy selection job must report the selected parent context, selected child context, final resource evidence ids, and the rejected sibling or decoy context.

locator.quote: selected parent context, selected child context, final resource evidence ids

items.text: Hierarchy selection remains blocked until OpenViking same-corpus retrieval covers every expected evidence id instead of only reaching setup and returning wrong_result.
ELF hierarchy or trace behavior may be compared only if the same hierarchy-selection scenario is encoded and produces comparable selected-node and rejected-node evidence.
Decoy: OpenViking should win hierarchy selection solely because its design uses viking:// hierarchy paths.

answer.content: OpenViking hierarchy selection is blocked until selected hierarchy nodes and evidence ids are materialized. OpenViking's hierarchy design remains a reference, not a scored win, tie, or loss, until comparable output exists.

claims.text: OpenViking hierarchy selection is blocked until selected hierarchy nodes and evidence ids are materialized.
OpenViking's hierarchy design remains a reference, not a scored win, tie, or loss, until comparable output exists.

timeline.summary: The fixture records the minimum hierarchy readback needed before scoring.
The benchmark blocks hierarchy selection scoring until same-corpus evidence ids match.
The benchmark requires comparable ELF and OpenViking hierarchy artifacts before any win/tie/loss.

prompt.content: Can the benchmark score OpenViking hierarchy selection quality against ELF?

must_include.text: OpenViking hierarchy selection is blocked until selected hierarchy nodes and evidence ids are materialized.
OpenViking's hierarchy design remains a reference, not a scored win, tie, or loss, until comparable output exists.

required_evidence.quote: selected parent context, selected child context, final resource evidence ids

answer_correctness.criteria: States hierarchy selection is blocked until output is materialized.

evidence_grounding.criteria: Cites hierarchy output requirements and same-corpus precondition evidence.

trap_avoidance.criteria: Does not convert design references into scored wins or losses.

uncertainty_handling.criteria: Keeps the hierarchy comparison caveated as blocked.

workflow_helpfulness.criteria: Names the selected-node artifact needed next.

hard_fail_rules: unsupported high-confidence claim about a required decision or fact
use of a negative trap marked failure_if_used = true
